Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps says it has targeted what it describes as “separatist groups” in the Kurdistan Region of Iraq.

title: Iran keeps targeting Gulf States despite president's apology
description:

Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps said it targeted U.S. forces at the Naval Support Activity Bahrain in Juffair on Saturday. Explosions were also heard over Dubai and the Qatari capital, Doha. Meanwhile, the president of the United Arab Emirates said the country would emerge stronger after the war.

title: Iranian civilians seek shelter abroad
description:
As strikes against Iran appear to intensify, increasing numbers of Iranians are seeking safety abroad, raising fears that the conflict could trigger a refugee crisis. The United Nations has warned of a “moment of grave peril” for civilians caught up in the fighting, urging de‑escalation and protection for vulnerable populations.

Nicholas Rushworth tells us more.

title: Israel's North residents asked to leave
description:
This Saturday, Iranian Army said it launched a new wave of drone attacks on Israel, specifically targeting what it called a “strategic facility.”

Explosions were heard in the skies above Jerusalem shortly after the military said its air defenses were working to intercept projectiles from Iran. No injuries were reported.

An army spokesman added that there had been a “real decrease” in the number of launches from Iran after nearly a week of barra

title: French citizens scramble to flee Gulf: Flight prices surge as thousands seek repatriation
description: As the conflict rages on, efforts to repatriate EU citizens stranded in the Middle East are ongoing. Among French nationals alone, around 5,000 are seeking to leave. Commercial flights across the region have been postponed, and the few tickets that remain available are extremely expensive. France 2’s Mathieu Rio and France 24’s Peter O’Brien have the story.

title: Concerns over international law violations mount
description:
On the eighth day of the joint US-Israeli military operation against Iran, questions are emerging about the legality of the Middle East war, though those directing the campaign do not seem overly concerned.

For more on the legal issues surrounding the conflict, Professor Marc Weller University, a law professor at Cambridge University, shares further insights.

title: Russia wins first Winter Paralympics medals since Sochi Games
description: The Russian flag made its first appearance at a Paralympics medal ceremony in 12 years on Saturday as Varvara Voronchikhina and Aleksei Bugaev took bronze in the women's and men's downhill standing events, earning a polite smattering of applause from the crowd in Cortina.

title: Israel carries out rare airbone raid in Lebanon
description: Lebanon’s Health Ministry said Israeli airstrikes during a commando operation in eastern Lebanon left at least 41 dead and 40 wounded. "The series of raids launched by the Israeli enemy on the town of Nabi Sheet and surrounding towns in the Baalbek district resulted in a total of 41 citizens killed and 40 others wounded," the ministry said in a statement.  FRANCE24 Senior Reporter Catherine Norris Trent has the latest from Beirut, Lebanon.

title: Israel says began new strikes on Iran's Tehran, Isfahan
description:
Over 80 Israeli warplanes carried out airstrikes across Iran overnight in the largest bombing campaign since the fighting began last Saturday. The Israeli military said the attacks targeted strategic infrastructure, including a military academy, an underground command center and a missile storage facility.

However, Iran’s ambassador to the United Nations accused both the United States and Israel of striking densely populated civilian areas, call

title: Displaced in Lebanon: 500,000 told to flee Dahiyeh in Beirut
description:
Over the past week, Israel has issued evacuation warnings before carrying out heavy strikes on southern Lebanon and the southern suburbs of Beirut.

Adéa Guillot, spokesperson for CARE France, says there are no precise figures for the total number of displaced people. description:
Over the past week, Israel has issued evacuation warnings before launching heavy strikes on southern Lebanon and the southern suburbs of Beirut. The IDF estimates that more than 300,000 Lebanese civilians have so far evacuated their villages in southern Lebanon.

UN Human Rights spokesperson Thameen Al-Kheetan provides further details on human rights violations since the start of the conflict.

title: Israeli strike eliminates Hamas commander in Lebanon
description:

His elimination adds to the growing number of Hamas and Palestinian Islamic Jihad members who have been killed in Lebanon by the Israeli military.

title: Sanaa's mass rally amid regional strikes: Houthis warn of escalation after death of Khamenei
description:
Amid regional strikes and reported death of Iran’s Supreme Leader, Sanaa in Yemen has emerged as a focal point of defiance. Yemen’s Houthi leader Abdul Malik al-Houthi declared support for Iran and said the group was ready to escalate militarily if necessary. He also urged supporters to stage mass demonstrations on Friday in Sanaa and other areas under the group’s control. 

Caitlin Brown reports.

title: Israel continues to strike Lebanon: At least 16 killed in raids on Nabi Chit
description:
Local media report that at least 26 people were killed overnight in Israeli airstrikes on the eastern town of Nabi Chit, a small mountain community in the Bekaa Valley with strong ties to Hezbollah’s leadership.

Over the past week, Israel issued evacuation orders before launching heavy strikes on southern Lebanon and the southern suburbs of Beirut. Hundreds of thousands of civilians have fled the area. Morgan Aire reports.

title: War in Middle East live: Trump threatens ‘very hard’ Iran strikes, warns of new targets
description: US President Donald Trump on Saturday said Iran would be "hit very hard", adding that he was considering expanding strikes to new targets, without providing details. Iran’s President Pezeshkian earlier announced that he would not “surrender” as demanded by Trump Friday and apologised to neighbouring countries for the strikes that targeted them. title: Russia sharing intelligence information to help Tehran, officials say

description:
In Iraq, Kurdish authorities are warning that their territory must not be used as a launchpad for attacks on Iran. Kurdish Iranian dissident groups say they will not act independently, but could potentially join a US ground invasion.
 
Iran has already carried out several missile and drone strikes on Kurdish bases, escalating tensions in the area. On Friday, Iranian drones were shot down over Erbil in Iraq’s semi-autonomous Kurdish region.

title: Death toll mounts in Middle East war: Over 1,400 killed across Iran, Lebanon and Israel

description: The conflict in Iran may give Moscow a short-term boost economically and in Ukraine. But it has also shown the limits of Russia’s partnerships.

title: Белый дом — о возможной помощи России в ударах по войскам США: «Это не важно»
description: Пресс-секретарь Белого дома Кэролайн Левитт не подтвердила, но и не опровергла информацию The Washington Post о том, что Россия может предоставлять разведданные Ирану для нанесения ударов по американским силам на Ближнем Востоке. Она заявила, что это все равно не имеет значения для Вашингтона, поскольку, уверила представитель администрации, американская армия продолжает достигать военных целей в операции против Ирана.

title: Azerbaijan says it foiled Iranian terror attacks on synagogue, Israeli embassy
description:
Authorities say at least seven Azerbaijani nationals detained; plot also included attack on pipeline servicing Israeli oil imports
The post Azerbaijan says it foiled Iranian terror attacks on synagogue, Israeli embassy appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Israel said backing Kurdish offensive in Iran, with sides in talks for a year
description:
Sources tell Reuters of 'long-term' contacts, with Kurdish militants poised to assault border territories and fight IRGC; senior PDKI official says group ready to join fight
The post Israel said backing Kurdish offensive in Iran, with sides in talks for a year appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Winter Paralympics officially open, Russian athletes greeted with boos
description: Some spectators booed Russia's athletes as they marched under their own flag in the opening ceremony of the Milan-Cortina Winter Paralympics on Friday. The International Paralympic Committee in February lifted a long-running ban on Russian and Belarusian athletes competing under their own flags and anthems due to their role in the ongoing war in Ukraine.

title: 51 people have been killed in the last 24 hours in Sudan's Kordofan region
description: First, 51 people have been killed in the last 24 hours in Sudan's Kordofan region due to clashes between the army and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces. Also, UN Secretary General Anotnio Guterres appoints Former US diplomat James Swan as the new head of the MONUSCO in DR Congo. And, we will take a look at the "WOMANAKWA" photo exhibition launched here in Paris.

title: Strikes on Iran to last ‘four to six weeks’, White House says
description: White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt says the US expects the war on Iran to last about four to six weeks”.

title: 'Massive' numbers killed by gunmen in latest Nigeria attack, senator tells BBC
description: Suspected Islamist militants reportedly seized control of a village for two days before being forced out by troops and air strikes.

title: South African President on Trump’s Oval Office ‘Ambush’ and ‘Racist’ Policies
description: In an interview with The New York Times, President Cyril Ramaphosa opened up about the role of middle powers, relations with Washington and apartheid.

title: Iran’s Friends Include China and Russia. But Where are They Now?
description: Iran maintains ties with a range of countries, including Turkey, India, Russia and China. Yet in this war, their support is mostly rhetoric.
